When TestRunResult is created via the simple constructor (e.g. on internal
test run failure), the Messages property is never initialized, causing
ArgumentNullException in AddRange. Use null-coalescing fallback to empty
collection.

covering the Aggregate path that previously threw ArgumentNullException.

The root cause of the ArgumentNullException is that the simple
constructor never initialized Messages, leaving it null. Fix it at the
source by initializing to an empty collection. Keep the ?? [] fallback
in Aggregate as defense in depth.

Add regression tests to verify Messages is never null from either
constructor path.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Deltares-research/cht_nesting/sessions/5ea20ad1-ad1e-4f09-8c08-f16e2caee832

Co-authored-by: maartenvanormondt <82946105+maartenvanormondt@users.noreply.github.com>

* removed all calls to cht_sfincs and cht_hurrywave

* Add hydromt_sfincs test dep; use naive timestamp

Include hydromt_sfincs in the project's optional test dependencies so tests that rely on it are available. Adjust tests/conftest.py to create a naive pandas.Timestamp (remove tz="UTC") for tref to avoid timezone-aware vs naive datetime arithmetic when computing tsec.

* Drop hydromt_sfincs from tests; mock details

Remove hydromt_sfincs from the tests extras in pyproject.toml and refactor test fixtures to avoid the external dependency. tests/conftest.py now provides a _make_detail_mock helper that builds a SfincsModel-like MagicMock (CRS, bounds PropertyMock, and water_level / snapwave_boundary_conditions get_locations/set_timeseries behavior) and uses it for detail_model_wl and detail_model_sw. Also added PropertyMock and pyproj.CRS imports and cleaned up a duplicate import in make_mock_beware. This allows running the test suite without installing hydromt_sfincs.

* Require hydromt_sfincs in tests and fixtures

Install hydromt_sfincs in CI and update test fixtures to use the real SfincsModel when available. The workflow now pip-installs the hydromt_sfincs repo during tests. conftest.py no longer mocks SfincsModel: it adds a runtime import/check (pytest.importorskip) and a skip marker for environments without hydromt_sfincs, removes the unused PropertyMock import, and replaces the mocked detail_model_wl/detail_model_sw with minimal real SfincsModel instances (grid created and boundary locations set). This ensures tests exercise actual hydromt_sfincs behavior while skipping when the dependency is absent.

* Add optional hydromt_sfincs install to CI

Introduce a matrix flag (install_hydromt) and a conditional workflow step to install hydromt_sfincs from the GitHub repo for full test runs. The flag is set for the Python 3.12/ubuntu job, and the new step runs `pip install git+https://github.com/Deltares-research/hydromt_sfincs.git@main` with a 10-minute timeout. This makes running tests that depend on hydromt_sfincs optional and controlled by the matrix configuration.

* Remove hydromt_sfincs install from CI

Remove the optional install_hydromt matrix flag and the conditional step that installed hydromt_sfincs in .github/workflows/tests.yml. Tests still create the conda env and run pytest; this simplifies the CI matrix by removing the extra dependency install and its timeout.

* Update tests.yml

* formatting and docs

commit.message:
Update #138 — compact layout, cleaner arcs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add HDR burst photography using OpenCV Mertens fusion

Capture a 3-frame exposure bracket (-1, 0, +1 EV), align frames with
AlignMTB and fuse them with MergeMertens (Mertens-Kautz-Van Reeth 2007).
Using QImage for file I/O avoids libopencv-imgcodecs and its heavy
libgdal/libgdcm transitive dependencies (~7 MB runtime vs ~1 GB dev deps).

Changes:
- src/hdrprocessor.h / .cpp: HdrProcessor QObject with Q_INVOKABLE
  processHdrBurst(); pipeline: load via QImage, AlignMTB align,
  MergeMertens fuse, float->uint8, save via QImage, EXIF copy via
  exiv2, burst frame cleanup. Output named imageYYYYMMDD_HHmmsszzz_hdr.jpg
  so files sort by date alongside regular photos in the gallery.
- CMakeLists.txt: find_package OpenCV (core/imgproc/photo), add new
  sources, link OpenCV libs.
- src/main.cpp: register HdrProcessor as FuriCam/HdrProcessor QML type.
- icons/hdrOn.svg + icons.qrc: HDR toggle button icon.
- src/qml/Camera.qml: signal photoSaved(); HdrProcessor instance; HDR
  burst state machine (hdrBurstActive, hdrFramePaths, hdrCaptureTimer,
  hdrEvSchedule); captureToLocation to .burst/ hidden subdir; onImageSaved
  collects frames and calls processHdrBurst when all frames are in.
- src/qml/main.qml: hdrEnabled setting; HDR toggle button in config bar;
  galleryRefreshTimer that calls mediaView.refresh() after each save;
  photoSaved signal wired in connectSignals/disconnectSignals.
- src/qml/MediaReview.qml: refresh() method using a _refreshClearing
  toggle + 50 ms Timer to force FolderListModel rescan across two
  event-loop turns (single-turn folder = '' / folder = path is batched
  as a no-op by the QML engine).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: apply council fixes for PR #30 a11y and UX issues

Fix 1: Drop role="list" from column list container (invalid ARIA ownership
  - role="list" requires role="listitem" children; buttons are not listitems)

Fix 2: Replace role="checkbox"+aria-checked with aria-pressed on column toggle
  buttons (native <button> fires on Space+Enter; checkbox pattern is Space-only,
  causing AT keyboard-contract mismatch with NVDA/JAWS)

Fix 3: Suppress native webkit search cancel button via CSS pseudo-element
  (::-webkit-search-cancel-button, ::-webkit-search-decoration) so only the
  custom .clear-search control is visible in Chrome/Edge

Fix 4: Wrap localStorage.setItem in try/catch in persistColumnVisibility to
  gracefully handle QuotaExceededError (Safari private mode, strict-quota
  scenarios); symmetric with loadColumnVisibility read-path guard

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ui): apply council round-2 fixes for PR #30 a11y

Round-2 post-fix audit identified two defects that round 1 missed, both
verified and applied via the Disclosure pattern:

- R2-1: ColumnDropdown declared role="dialog" with none of the three
  WAI-ARIA 1.2 section 6.6 mandatory keyboard behaviors (focus-on-open,
  Tab containment, focus-return-on-close). Toolbar trigger declared
  aria-haspopup="dialog" making an AT promise the implementation did
  not keep. Swapped to the Disclosure pattern: removed role="dialog"
  and aria-label from ColumnDropdown, removed aria-haspopup from the
  Toolbar trigger (aria-expanded remains and is sufficient for the
  Disclosure Button pattern).

- R2-2: Dead panel binding left behind by commit 5a9e382 when it
  removed the onMount block that consumed it. Removed the let panel
  declaration and the bind:this={panel} on the outer div.

Also dropped the stale onClose={onCloseColumns} prop passed to
ColumnDropdown (the ColumnDropdown onClose prop was removed in the
round-1 fix commit, leaving the call site stale).

Verified: svelte-check clean (0 errors, 0 warnings), vite build green.

Ref: .council/council-recommendation.md (round 2)
Ref: .council/2026-04-10-council-recommendation-round1.md (round 1)

* fix(ui): apply council round-3 fixes for PR #30 a11y and UX

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ui): apply council session-4 fixes for PR #30 a11y

- Replace role="dialog" with role="region" on ColumnDropdown (S4-1)
  Disclosure trigger uses aria-expanded; region label transfers correctly;
  no focus-trap required for non-modal panel widget
- Sync aria-label on search button to dynamic searchTitle (S4-2)
  Filter and columns buttons were updated in session 3; search was missed

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ui): apply council session-5 fix for PR #30 a11y

Add role="img" to sort indicator div in Toolbar.svelte so aria-label
is effective on screen readers. Plain <div> elements without a role
silently drop aria-label; lucide SVG icons are aria-hidden by default,
leaving AT users with no way to read the current sort state.

Finding S5-1 (medium a11y) — verified closed.
Finding S5-2 (missing aria-controls, low) — recorded as follow-up.
svelte-check: 0 errors 0 warnings.

* fix(ui): add aria-controls linkage for column disclosure (PR #30 S5-2)

Add id="columns-panel" to ColumnDropdown root and aria-controls="columns-panel"
to the columns trigger button. Completes the WAI-ARIA APG Disclosure Button
pattern so AT users can programmatically jump from trigger to the disclosed region.

Finding S5-2 closed. All 12 council findings fixed across 5 sessions.

* fix(ui): correct searchTitle derivation — label now matches toggleSearch action (PR #30 S6)

searchTitle was derived from searchActive (searchVisible || searchQuery.length > 0),
but toggleSearch() branches only on searchVisible. When the bar is collapsed with an
active search term, the button label said "Close search" while the action opened the bar.
Fixed by deriving searchTitle from searchVisible alone; the .active class already
communicates that a filter is running to sighted users.

* fix(ui): apply council session-7 fixes for PR #30

- Wire searchVisible prop into Toolbar.svelte (S7-1 HIGH: unbound
  variable caused searchTitle to always read 'Open search' even
  when the bar was already open)
- Remove dead webkit search-cancel-button CSS (input is type=text
  since session 3, selectors were permanently inactive)

All 14 findings across sessions 1–7 are now fixed. svelte-check
passes 0 errors 0 warnings.

* fix(ui): apply council session-8 fixes for PR #30 a11y

S8-1 (MEDIUM): Three-way aria-label on search button — now reads
"Search active — open search bar (Ctrl+K)" when a query is active
but the panel is closed, so AT users know rows are filtered.
Previously always read "Open search" regardless of active state.

S8-2 (LOW, optional): Remove dead CSS transition on .search-panel —
{#if} removes the DOM node so CSS transitions never fire; translateY(0)
was a no-op against the CSS default anyway.

* chore(council): record session 8 — 2 findings, all fixed, PR #30 clean

S8-1 MEDIUM: search button AT label missing active-filter indication
S8-2 LOW: dead CSS transition on .search-panel removed

All 16 findings (S1-S8) are fixed. Council verdict: FIXED.

Filter button was missing aria-expanded={filtersVisible}; search and
columns buttons both carry the attribute. Carry-forward miss from
session 9 which fixed the search button (F17) but left the filter
button without programmatic expanded/collapsed state per WCAG 4.1.2.
aria-controls waived — no id target exists (filter inputs render
inline in RevoGrid column headers).

aria-controls="search-panel" was wired to Toolbar.svelte in S9 but the
matching id attribute on the search panel div in App.svelte was not committed.

Session 9: F17 (search button aria-expanded/aria-controls + search-panel id),

Session 11: zero findings. APPROVED unconditionally. 19 total findings across

commit.message:
Fix [AUDIT-093]: Use close-time offsets for queue admission and nomination trimming

Queue admission now rejects transactions whose max_time will expire before
the estimated next ledger close, using upper_bound_offset = expected_close * 2
+ drift (parity: stellar-core getUpperBoundCloseTimeOffset).

Nomination tx-set building now computes close_time BEFORE building the tx set
and passes the offset to trimming (parity: stellar-core triggerNextLedger
computes nextCloseTime first, then passes offset to makeTxSetFromTransactions).

commit.message:
refactor: range-based message provider (IDB cache + tentacle fallback)

Replace dual-path requestLatest/requestBefore with single fetchRange()
method. Callers request a seq range; provider checks IDB first, falls
back to tentacle for missing messages, delivers complete result in one
store write. No gap possible.

- Add getMessagesInRange() to message-db (IDB range query on compound key)
- Rewrite MessageProvider with fetchRange + pending Promise for tentacle
- handleBatch resolves pending request or falls back to direct store write
- Update handleSessionList, GapMarker, and session_created fork handler
- Fix gap bug: messages between IDB lastSeq and totalLastSeq-50 are no

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: fix prototype chain IC regression + phi copy elimination

- Remove broken proto_handle validation from all 3 proto IC hit paths.
  The validation compared DIRECT prototype shape against the shape of
  the object where the property was found (N levels up). For chains
  with depth > 1, these never matched, causing every IC hit to fail
  and fall through to the slow path (145x regression).
  The receiver shape + global epoch check is sufficient.

- Partition phi copy operations into safe (direct MOV) and conflicting
  (push/pop via stack). Safe entries skip MOV when src==dst register.
  Eliminates redundant stack round-trips at loop back-edges.
